title: Star Wars: Clone Wars
content:

'''''Star Wars: Clone Wars''''' is an animated television micro-series that chronicles the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios, the series originally aired from [[2003]] to [[2005]], and was intended to serve as a bridge between the films [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. The series consists of twenty-five chapters; Seasons 1 and 2 (Volume I) are made up of three-minute installments, while Season 3 (Volume II) is made up of twelve-to-fifteen minute installments. The success of ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' led to production of the similarly titled ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''.

==Development==
[[File:CloneWarsPoster.jpg|thumb|left|200px]]
In the early 2000s, [[Hasbro]] asked [[Lucasfilm]] to develop something between [[Prequel trilogy|prequel films]],<ref name="Stormtrooper">''[[Star Wars: Stormtroopers: Beyond the Armor]]''</ref> which they hoped would boost action figure sales and help promote the upcoming [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.browndailyherald.com/2008/11/17/cartoon-network-animator-traces-his-path-to-the-top|text=Cartoon Network animator traces his path to the top|author=Sedano, Caroline|date=2008-11-17|archivedate=20200803143601}}</ref><ref>''Total Film'' magazine, May 2008 issue, p. 137</ref> [[Cartoon Network]]—who had previously worked with Hasbro on their [[Wikipedia:Transformers|''Transformers'']] series—suggested [[Genndy Tartakovsky]], who was finishing work on the animated series [[Wikipedia:Samurai Jack|''Samurai Jack'']]. Lucasfilm met with Tartakovsky,<ref name="Stormtrooper" /> who agreed to the project if the shorts could be extended from the original proposed 1-minute runtime up to 3-to-5 minutes.<ref name="blastr">{{WebCite|url=https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/how-the-clone-wars-microseries-led-the-way-for-star-wars-return-to-tv|text=How the Clone Wars microseries led the way for Star Wars' return to TV|author=Granshaw, Lisa|date=2015-04-29|archivedate=20190609001042}}</ref>

[[File:CW_Title.png|thumb|right|200px|''Clone Wars'' opening logo]]
Tartakovsky pitched the show as having "a [[Wikipedia:Band of Brothers|''Band of Brothers'']]-feel to it—where it's episodes of different battles and strategies during the Clone Wars."<ref>{{HBCite|79}}</ref> After Lucasfilm and Cartoon Network approved the idea, Tartakovsky produced twenty 3-minute episodes.<ref name="SWArchive-Three-Minute Epics: A Look at Star Wars: Clone Wars">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/f20030220/index.html|text=Three-Minute Epics: A Look at ''Star Wars: Clone Wars''}}</ref> The series was produced using mostly [[Wikipedia:Traditional animation|traditional animation]] techniques.<ref>{{HBCite|92}}</ref> For example, Tartakovsky animated C-3PO's eyes in a way that pays homage to the animation style of [[Nelvana]], who produced the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Droids'']] and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] series and ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''{{'s}} [[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee|animated segment]]—all of which he had grown up with.<ref>{{HBCite|97}}</ref>

The series's success led to the greenlight of a third season consisting of five 12- to 15-minute episodes.<ref name="SWArchive-The Clone Wars Micro-Series Continues!">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20040608.html|text=The ''Clone Wars'' Micro-Series Continues!}}</ref> Cartoon Network held a poll to determine which of three new Jedi should be introduced in the series. The three choices were [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]], [[Voolvif Monn]], and [[Foul Moudama]]. Voolvif Monn won the vote and was introduced in "[[Chapter 20 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 20]]." The other two Jedi were later introduced anyway, both of whom played parts larger than Monn's.<ref name="poll">{{CN|url=promos/200311_jedi/poll.html|text=Cartoon Network Poll}}</ref><ref name="SWArchive-Gentle Giant Toons Out the Original Trilogy">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/gentlegiant/news20060306.html|text=Gentle Giant Toons Out the Original Trilogy}}</ref>

==Release and reception==
''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' ran on Cartoon Network from [[November 7]], 2003 to [[March 25]], [[2005]]. The episodes were released simultaneously on television and online for members of the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace fan club]]. They were available the next day on cartoonnetwork.com and on [[StarWars.com]] for non-Hyperspace members.<ref name="Clone Wars Home on Hyperspace">{{SWArchive|url=hyperspace/about/news/news20031024.html|text=''Clone Wars'' Home on Hyperspace}}</ref> In an act of cross-promotion, Cartoon Network ran a ''Star Wars'' contest involving the [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|2004 DVD release]] of the [[original trilogy]].<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/holonet/story/Cartoon_Network_SW_Trilogy_Contest_88294.asp|text=Cartoon Network SW Trilogy Contest|date=2004-12-11|archivedate=20200609164303}}</ref>

[[20th Century Studios|20th Century Fox]] released the series on [[Star Wars home video releases|home video]] in two phases in 2005; The [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|first DVD]] was released on [[March 22]], 2005 (one day after the third and final season began airing) and contained Chapters 1 through 20.<ref name="SWArchive-Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume 1 Coming to DVD">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20041206.html|text=''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume 1 Coming to DVD}}</ref> The [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two|second DVD]] was released on [[December 6]], 2005 (the same day as the "family pack" DVD re-release of the original trilogy) and contained Chapters 21 through 25.<ref name="SWArchive-Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume 2 Coming to DVD">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20050905.html|text=''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume 2 Coming to DVD}}</ref> Chapters 1 and 8 were also included on a "Bonus Lightsaber Action DVD" that was packaged with Hasbro's Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Darth Vader toy lightsabers.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/bonus_dvd_with_lightsaber_89452.asp|text=Bonus DVD With Lightsaber|author=Roberts, Dustin|date=2005-02-10|archivedate=20200610020940}}</ref>

In [[March]], [[2021]], it was announced on [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney's]] website D23.com that the entire series would be available on [[Disney+]] starting April 2.<ref name="Disney+">{{D23|url=everything-new-you-can-stream-on-disney-in-april-2021|text=Everything New You Can Stream on Disney+ in April 2021|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Adaptations and tie-in media==
A [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Cine-Manga|PhotoComic adaptation]] of the entire series was released on [[May 21]], 2008.<ref name="SWArchive-Upcoming Dark Horse Titles for Spring 2008">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20080220.html|text=Upcoming Dark Horse Titles for Spring 2008}}</ref> [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] released a [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|comic book series]] based on the artwork and atmosphere of the show, in some cases with direct plot tie-ins. Hasbro released three waves of action figures—one for each season. The first two were initially available exclusively at [[Wikipedia:Target Corporation|Target]], and then later at [[StarWars.com|StarWarsShop.com]], whereas the final wave was released at multiple retailers.<ref name="Wave 1 Figures">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/hasbro/news20031029.html|text=Clone Wars Animated Figures}}</ref><ref name="Wave 2 Figures>{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/hasbro/news20031209.html|text=Clone Wars Animated Figures - Wave 2}}</ref><ref name="SWShop">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/shop/shopnews/news20040802.html|text=Animated Clone Wars Figures at StarWarsShop.com}}</ref><ref name="Wave 3 Figures">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/hasbro/news20041118.html|text=Cartoon Network ''Clone Wars'' Figures Coming This Spring}}</ref> In July 2021, following the Disney+ release of the show, StarWars.com announced that Hasbro would be producing ''Clone Wars'' figures for its Black Series and Vintage Collection lines.<ref name="New Figures">{{SW|url=news/clone-wars-2d-micro-series-figures|text=New Black Series and Vintage Collection Figures to Celebrate the ''Clone Wars 2D Micro-Series''}}</ref>

==Reception {{SectionFlag}}==
''Clone Wars'' was one of the first online series to win an Emmy Award, due to the fact that it was released on television and the internet simultaneously.<ref name="Clone Wars Home on Hyperspace" /> Seasons one and two won an Emmy award for "Outstanding Animated Program" in 2004,<ref name="SWArchive-Clone Wars Micro-Series Wins Emmy Award">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20040913.html|text=''Clone Wars'' Micro-Series Wins Emmy Award}}</ref> and season three won in 2005 for the same category.<ref name="SWArchive-Clone Wars Volume 2 Wins Emmy Award">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20050919.html|text=''Clone Wars'' Volume 2 Wins Emmy Award}}</ref> Background key designer [[Justin Thompson]] also won in "Outstanding Individual Achievement in Animation" for his work on season three.<ref name="SWArchive-Clone Wars Volume 2 Wins Juried Emmy Award">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/about/news/news20050818.html|text=''Clone Wars'' Volume 2 Wins Juried Emmy Award}}</ref> The series was also a nominee and winner, respectively, of the 32nd and 33rd Annie Awards for "Best Animated Television Production."<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://annieawards.org/32nd-annie-awards|text=32nd Annual Annie Nominations and Awards Recipients|archivedate=20200901145240}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=https://annieawards.org/33rd-annie-awards|text=33rd Annual Annie Nominations and Awards Recipients|archivedate=20200609190825}}</ref>
